elections.note: CA SoS per-county reporting-status page ('FENU'-coded row), San Diego County: 2,328 of 2,328 precincts (100.0%) reporting, Registered 1,488,157, Ballots Cast 980,234, First Report 'Nov 4 8:01 p.m.', Latest Report 'Nov 5 6:46 a.m.' (Report Type FENU), at Wayback capture 20081106063312 (2008-11-06 06:33:12 UTC = Nov 5, 2008 22:33 PST), page header '100.0% (25,423 of 25,423) precincts partially or fully reporting as of Nov. 5, 2008, at 10:05 p.m.'. Bracket (RUNBOOK 8 leg): the next capture 20081108032433 (2008-11-08 03:24:33 UTC, page header 'as of Nov. 7, 2008, at 7:06 p.m.') shows San Diego's row grown to 1,051,756 ballots cast (70.7%), Latest Report now 'Nov 7 5:04 p.m.', Report Type changed FENU->CCU (county canvass update) -- a clean non-circular bracket proving 980,234 was San Diego's last FENU/election-night figure before the canvass moved past it. NOT the first-tranche figure (the page's separate 'First Report' column, 'Nov 4 8:01 p.m.', was not used). Certified final 1,245,947 Total Voters (CA SoS Statement of Vote, Voter Participation Statistics by County, https://elections.cdn.sos.ca.gov/sov/2008-general/3_voter_part_stats_by_county.pdf, Precinct Voters 672,778 + Vote-By-Mail Voters 573,169 = 1,245,947 exact). Arithmetic: 980,234/1,245,947 = 78.67%. vs_epollbook=pre, vs_asv=pre (SD adopted e-pollbooks 2022, ASV 2024). Grade CONFIRMED per RUNBOOK 8 (self-describing FENU code plus the status-page version bracket). Recovered via docs/research/night-recovery-2026-07-11-san-diego/wayback-probe-1994-2010.md (2008-11-04 section); independently re-verified here (2026-07-12) by re-fetching both raw id_ captures directly (plain HTML, not gzip-encoded) and confirming San Diego's row and the bracket figure byte-for-byte against the probe's transcription.

CA SoS per-county reporting-status page ('FENU'-coded row), San Diego County: 2,050 of 2,050 precincts (100%) reporting, Registered 1,442,161, Ballots Cast 663,326, First Report 'Nov 2 8:02 p.m.', Latest Report 'Nov 3 2:22 a.m.' (Report Type FENU), at Wayback capture 20101105215804 (2010-11-05 21:58:04 UTC = Nov 5, 2010 13:58 PST), page header '100.0% (24,845 of 24,845) precincts partially or fully reporting as of November 5, 2010, 9:04 a.m.'. Bracket (RUNBOOK 8 leg): the next capture 20101110011312 (2010-11-10 01:13:12 UTC) shows San Diego's row grown to 825,597 ballots cast (57.2%), Latest Report now 'Nov 8 5:04 p.m.', Report Type changed FENU->CCU -- the same clean non-circular bracket mechanic as the 2008 row, proving 663,326 was San Diego's last election-night figure before the canvass moved past it. NOT the first-tranche figure (the page's separate 'First Report' column, 'Nov 2 8:02 p.m.', was not used). Certified final 926,363 Total Voters (CA SoS Statement of Vote, Voter Participation Statistics by County, https://elections.cdn.sos.ca.gov/sov/2010-general/03-voter-particpation-stats-by-county.pdf [filename misspells 'particpation'], Precinct Voters 416,188 + Vote-By-Mail Voters 510,175 = 926,363 exact; the same PDF's Registered Voters column, 1,442,161, matches the status page's 'Reg'd Voters' figure exactly, an independent cross-check). Arithmetic: 663,326/926,363 = 71.61%. vs_epollbook=pre, vs_asv=pre (SD adopted e-pollbooks 2022, ASV 2024). Grade CONFIRMED per RUNBOOK 8. Recovered via docs/research/night-recovery-2026-07-11-san-diego/wayback-probe-1994-2010.md (2010-11-02 section); independently re-verified here (2026-07-12) by re-fetching both raw id_ captures directly (plain HTML, not gzip-encoded) and confirming San Diego's row and the bracket figure byte-for-byte against the probe's transcription, and by independently sourcing/fetching the 2010 SoS SoV Voter Participation Statistics PDF for the denominator (the probe did not source denominators).

Certified final (denominator) SOLID: CA SoS SoV Voter Participation Statistics by County (filename literally '03-voter-reg-stats-by-county.pdf' despite containing the participation table -- do not confuse with the true registration-stats '02-...' file in the same dir), San Diego Total Voters 548,462 (Precinct 187,942 + VBM 360,520). Election-night NUMERATOR not sourceable, checked fresh for the June date (not just inherited from the Nov-2012 general's dead end): (1) the sdvote.com/content/rov/electioninfo/election.xml feed that recovered the 2016 primary's plateau has ZERO Wayback captures anywhere 2010-2016 -- not archived until 2016; (2) a sdvote.com domain sweep for 2012-06-04 to 06-08 shows only robots.txt captured, no results page; (3) County News Center did not exist yet in 2012 (per the 2012-general row); (4) the registrar's own news page is archived only from 2019 on. No morning-after 'semi-final results' release or precise news figure found. Left null per RUNBOOK 5.1 rather than substitute a different report-time or denominator. Pre-epollbook (2022) and pre-ASV (2024).

Certified final (denominator) is SOLID: CA SoS Statement of Vote, Voter Participation Statistics by County, San Diego Total Voters 1,203,265 (= Precinct 528,258 + VBM 675,007). Election-night NUMERATOR not sourceable: San Diego did NOT use the livevoterturnout.com live-results system until 2018 (earliest archived SD results page is the June 2018 primary), and the 2012 ROV live-results page (a JS viewer of sdvote.com/content/rov/electioninfo/election.xml) was not captured by Wayback on election night (Nov 6-7 2012) in any renderable form. News coverage gives no precise election-night ballots-counted total. Per the metric definition, left null rather than substitute a different report-time or denominator. Pre-epollbook (SD adopted e-pollbooks in 2022) and pre-ASV (2024). PRESS-RELEASE ROUTE ALSO CHECKED (updated methodology) and is EMPTY for 2012: the registrar's own news page (sdvote.com/content/rov/en/elections/news.html) is only archived in Wayback from 2019 onward, and the County News Center (sandiegocounty.gov news, launched ~2014) did not yet exist in Nov 2012 — so no morning-after 'semi-official results' press release stating an election-night ballots-counted total is sourceable. Remains null.

Certified final (denominator) SOLID: CA SoS SoV Voter Participation Statistics by County, San Diego Total Voters 420,700 (Precinct 113,714 + VBM 306,986). Election-night NUMERATOR not sourceable, checked fresh for the June date: (1) the sdvote.com/content/rov/electioninfo/election.xml feed (the route that recovered 2016) has ZERO Wayback captures anywhere 2010-2016; (2) a sdvote.com domain sweep for 2014-06-02 to 06-06 shows only PRE-election static pages/PDFs (voter-reg forms, a candidate-list PDF captured 6/6) under the legacy voters/Eng/... URL structure, no live-results page or ballots-counted figure; (3) County News Center (existed by 2014) shows exactly one election-adjacent page in the window, a pre-election 'where to vote' reminder (2014-06-05), not a results release; (4) the registrar's own news page is archived only from 2019 on. No morning-after 'semi-final results' release or precise news figure found. Left null per RUNBOOK 5.1 rather than substitute a different report-time or denominator. Pre-epollbook (2022) and pre-ASV (2024).

CA SoS per-county reporting-status page ('FENU'-coded row), San Diego County: 100.0% precincts reporting, Ballots Cast 509,214, Last Report stamp 'Nov 5 1:14 a.m.' (First Report 'Nov 4 8:03 p.m.') at Wayback capture 20141105141649 (2014-11-05 14:16:49 UTC). SINGLE CAPTURE at 2014-11-05 14:16:49 UTC carries this value. The next available capture (2014-11-07 08:38:01 UTC) jumps to 541,370 (Last Report 'Nov 6 5:05 p.m.'), confirming 509,214 was election night's last state before the canvass moved (non-circular bracket leg per RUNBOOK 8). NOT the pre-plateau first-tranche row (page's own early-evening captures, <99% precincts reporting, were excluded per RUNBOOK 1 and the grading discipline in scratchpad sos-status-sweep.md). Certified final 692,434 Total Voters (CA SoS Statement of Vote, Voter Participation Statistics by County, https://elections.cdn.sos.ca.gov/sov/2014-general/pdf/03-voter-particpiation-stats-by-county.pdf). Arithmetic: 509,214/692,434 = 73.54%. vs_epollbook=pre, vs_asv=pre (per county_tech/adoption record: epollbook 2022, asv 2024). Grade CONFIRMED per RUNBOOK 8. Sourced from the 2026-07 CA SoS status-page sweep across the 19-county panel (scratchpad/sos-status-sweep.md, 2014-11-04 section); the cited capture and freeze/jump evidence above were independently re-derived here by matching this exact Ballots Cast figure against the raw per-capture status-page timeline (script scratchpad/build_cell_details.py), not merely transcribed from the sweep's prose summary.

PLATEAU metric, recovered from the lead flagged in the 2016 GENERAL row's own note ('Wayback captured election.xml only for the June 2016 primary'). Pre-livevoterturnout era GEMS-style XML at sdvote.com/content/rov/electioninfo/election.xml (plain text, curl-fetched, no rendering needed). Only election-night-adjacent Wayback capture is 20160608190823 (archived 6/8/2016 12:08pm PDT), whose internal ELECTION header reads date=06-08-16 time=03:21:51 etype=UNOFFICIAL ToBeCnt=285000 -- a 3:21am overnight report, matching the overnight-report pattern of every other SD row in this dataset. UNITED STATES SENATOR (CA's nonpartisan top-two blanket-primary contest, open to all voters regardless of party) shows tcounted=468340, a figure that recurs identically across ~15 other universal contests in the same file, confirming it is a genuine countywide ballot snapshot. The primary independent leg is growth in the next available capture (20160620034049, internal date 06-16-16 16:10:26): ToBeCnt fallen to 83,000 and US SENATOR tcounted risen to 689,612 -- i.e. the count grew substantially by the next capture (12 days later), proving 468,340 was the pre-canvass election-night figure, not the settled total (runbook 8 leg: report series' next file being days later, at a higher count). Secondary, weaker corroboration: the capture itself (12:08pm) is ~9h after the internal report time (3:21am) yet shows the same data, i.e. the live page had not updated in that intraday span. CONFIRMED. Sanity check: 468,340 + ToBeCnt 285,000 = 753,340, within ~3% of the certified final 775,930. 468,340/775,930 = 60.36%. Content-verified 2026-07-10 (tcounted="468340" present in the fetched XML). Pre-epollbook (2022) and pre-ASV (2024).

Certified final (denominator) is SOLID: CA SoS Statement of Vote, Voter Participation Statistics by County, San Diego Total Voters 1,346,513 (= Precinct 489,576 + VBM 856,937). Election-night NUMERATOR not sourceable: SD did not use livevoterturnout.com until 2018. In 2016 the ROV homepage 'Election Results' link pointed to a data feed sdvote.com/content/rov/electioninfo/election.xml consumed by a JS viewer, but Wayback captured election.xml only for the June 2016 primary and again starting 11/18/2016 (mid-canvass) — NOT on election night (Nov 8-9 2016). A broad domain crawl of sdvote.com for Nov 8-10 2016 shows only the homepage + static assets, no live-results page. News (KPBS 11/11/2016) reports '>500,000 ballots still left' as of Nov 11 (a canvass figure, not the election-night plateau). Left null rather than substitute. Pre-epollbook (2022) and pre-ASV (2024). PRESS-RELEASE ROUTE ALSO CHECKED (updated methodology) and is EMPTY: the County News Center's Nov-2016 monthly archive (Wayback ts 20161114190143, http://www.countynewscenter.com/2016/11/) lists only non-election items plus a single 'Election results available at SDVote.com' pointer (a link to the live page, NOT a release stating a ballots-counted total); the ROV's own news page (sdvote.com/.../news.html) is archived only from 2019. The live election.xml feed independently confirms NO election-night capture (captures cluster around the June-2016 primary, then nothing until the 11/18/2016 canvass). Remains null.

PLATEAU metric. San Diego's June 2018 primary is livevoterturnout.com/SanDiego/LiveResults/en/Index_3.html (Nov 2018 general = Index_5). Only election-night-window Wayback capture is 20180606114855 (4:48:55am PDT 6/6), which shows internal 'Website Updated: 6/6/2018 04:44:12 AM' and the page's own header 'UNOFFICIAL RESULTS - ELECTION NIGHT FINAL', Ballots Cast 406,501. The next capture (20180608031615, 6/7 8:16pm PDT, ~37.5h later) shows a different internal timestamp (6/7/2018 5:15:43 PM) and a higher count (427,779), confirming 406,501 was the pre-canvass election-night figure, superseded only by a later canvass update. CONFIRMED (self-description + later-capture-growth legs). 406,501/673,640 = 60.34%. Content-verified 2026-07-10 (406,501 present in the fetched, rendered page). Pre-epollbook (2022) and pre-ASV (2024).

PLATEAU metric (final election-night report). San Diego's live ENR page (livevoterturnout.com/SanDiego/LiveResults/en/Index_5.html) carries an internal 'Website Updated' data timestamp. The only election-night-era Wayback capture, taken 11/7/2018 04:06 PST (Wayback ts 20181107120607), renders 'Website Updated: 11/7/2018 01:42:41 AM' with 'Ballots Cast 536,734' and PRECINCTS REPORTING 1,446 of 2,136 (68%) — i.e. the last post-midnight election-night update, which was still the latest data as of the 4:06 AM snapshot (counting had stopped for the night). The next captures show the canvass had resumed: 11/9/2018 5:25 PM update = 728,420 (100% precincts), 11/13/2018 5:01 PM = 856,311. So 536,734 is the election-night plateau. 536,734 / 1,173,924 certified = 45.72%. NOTE the comparatively low share: 2018 was the precinct-voting era (no vote centers / e-pollbooks yet), and SD had only 68% of election-day precincts counted overnight, with the large vote-by-mail backlog (postmark-by-eday mail) counted during the multi-day canvass. This is a genuine plateau, NOT an 8 p.m. first tranche (which would be ~25-30%). Denominator = CA SoS Voter Participation Statistics by County (Total Voters 1,173,924 = Precinct 369,655 + VBM 804,269). Pre-epollbook (2022) and pre-ASV (2024). Snapshot used: Wayback 20181107120607, internal data ts 11/7/2018 01:42:41 AM.

PLATEAU metric. ROLLOUT-TIMING: this is the FIRST election on SD's ENR/vote-center/e-pollbook platform (ElectionID 15; the Nov 2022 general, already CONFIRMED elsewhere in this file, is ElectionID 16, immediately next); data/research/county-tech/san-diego-ca.json independently confirms both epollbook and vote-center adopted_year 2022, first_election '2022-06' = this primary. So vs_epollbook=post for THIS row too, not just Nov 2022. Index_15.html (Wayback 20220608181901, PDT 11:19am 6/8) shows internal 'Website Updated: 6/8/2022 12:39:38 AM' (post-midnight election-night update) with Ballots Cast 416,748 and 'NEXT POST 6/9/22 BY 5PM' still posted hours later with no intervening update -- the county's own posting-schedule bracket (runbook 8 leg). Corroborating leg: summary_15.xml (plain XML feed, 3 Wayback captures 2022-06-10) all show GeneratedDate 2022-06-09T16:49:11-07:00 / TotalBallotsCast 475,054 -- the very next (post-night, 6/9 5pm) report, exactly matching the promised schedule and confirming 416,748 held through the night (runbook 8 leg: report series' next file being days later). 416,748/674,608 = 61.78%. CONFIRMED. vs_asv=pre (ASV first-use year 2024 per county-tech record).

PLATEAU metric (final election-night report). Vote-center / e-pollbook era; the ENR page shows 'Ballots Cast' with an internal 'Website Updated' data timestamp. Sequence from Wayback captures of Index_16.html: 11/8/2022 8:01:54 PM = 487,957 (first tranche, pre-Election-Day mail + early vote-center); the count rose and the final election-night update was 11/9/2022 2:21:25 AM = 565,982. That 2:21 AM figure HELD steady across the 11/9 evening (ts 20221110062009) and 11/10 12:53 AM (ts 20221110085353) snapshots — SD posted no daytime-Nov-9 update — before the multi-day canvass resumed on 11/10/2022 4:58:34 PM = 649,443. So 565,982 is the election-night plateau. 565,982 / 1,043,490 certified = 54.24% ('PROJECTED OUTSTANDING BALLOTS: 500,000' shown alongside; 565,982 + 500,000 ~= certified). Well above the ~46.8% 8 p.m. first tranche, so it is the plateau, not the first report. Denominator = CA SoS Voter Participation Statistics by County (San Diego* Total Voters 1,043,490 = Precinct 104,388 + VBM 939,102). Post-epollbook (SD adopted e-pollbooks in 2022) and pre-ASV (2024; SD added automated signature verification in 2024). Snapshot used: Wayback 20221109172128, internal data ts 11/9/2022 2:21:25 AM.

PLATEAU metric, best-evidenced row in this dataset's primary set. ENR page Index_19.html (ElectionID 19, confirmed by its March 6 election-night captures and 'Presidential Primary Election' page title) literally headers itself 'FINAL UNOFFICIAL ELECTION NIGHT RESULTS' with internal 'Website Updated: 3/6/2024 12:46:42 AM' and 'NEXT UPDATE: MAR. 7' at the Wayback 20240306103630 capture (2:36am PST 3/6), Ballots Counted 425,572. That exact count+timestamp HELD across two later same-day captures (20240306145646 6:56am, 20240306223911 2:39pm, +14h) with no intervening update, then the 20240308170624 capture (3/8) shows Website Updated 3/7/2024 4:48:18 PM / Ballots Counted 492,333 -- the canvass resumed exactly on the promised MAR.7 schedule. Triple-legged CONFIRMED (self-description + held-across-captures + county posting-schedule bracket, all independently satisfied per runbook 8). 425,572/704,068 = 60.44%. vs_epollbook=post (2022 adoption). vs_asv: data/research/county-tech/san-diego-ca.json's ASV record (adopted_year 2024, confidence secondary) states VERBATIM: 'Could not pin first election to March 2024 primary vs Nov 2024 general from primary sources.' Recorded here as vs_asv='post' per the coarse adopted_year=2024 rule and to match the existing 2024-11-05 general row's convention, but the ambiguity is UNRESOLVED and carried forward: this row's vs_asv should be reconsidered if a more precise ASV go-live date is later found. NOTE FOR HUMAN REVIEW: the vs_asv classification (not the plateau, which is solidly evidenced above) is a judgment call the maintainer should double check.

PLATEAU metric (final election-night report) — matches the task's worked example. ENR page Index_20.html shows 'Ballots Counted' with an internal 'Website Updated' data timestamp. Sequence from Wayback captures: 11/5/2024 8:30:32 PM = 855,948 (first tranche, pre-Eday mail + early vote-center); the count rose and the final election-night update was 11/6/2024 2:52:20 AM = 975,373. That 975,373 HELD steady across the 11/6 evening (ts 20241107040913) and 11/7 11:52 AM (ts 20241107214306) snapshots before the multi-day canvass resumed on 11/7/2024 5:26:42 PM = 1,038,281. So 975,373 is the election-night plateau. 975,373 / 1,503,018 certified = 64.89% (consistent with the worked example's 64.9%; taking the 855,948 first tranche would wrongly give 56.9%). 'ESTIMATED BALLOTS TO BE PROCESSED: 590,000' shown alongside. Denominator = CA SoS Voter Participation Statistics by County (San Diego* Total Voters 1,503,018 = Precinct 227,714 + VBM 1,275,304). Post-epollbook (2022) and post-ASV (SD deployed automated signature verification in 2024). Snapshot used: Wayback 20241106165220, internal data ts 11/6/2024 2:52:20 AM.

notes: METRIC: election-night % = (San Diego County's FINAL election-night report, the cumulative ballots-counted/cast total that plateaus late on election night / just past midnight and HOLDS steady through the next day before the multi-day canvass resumes) / CA SoS certified final ballots (Statement of Vote, Voter Participation Statistics by County, 'Total Voters'). 2020 GENERAL deliberately excluded (COVID early-voting outlier). DENOMINATORS for all 14 rows are PRIMARY, taken directly from the CA SoS county-participation PDFs (San Diego Total Voters: 2008-11=1,245,947; 2010-11=926,363; 2012-06=548,462; 2012-11=1,203,265; 2014-06=420,700; 2014-11=692,434; 2016-06=775,930; 2016-11=1,346,513; 2018-06=673,640; 2018-11=1,173,924; 2022-06=674,608; 2022-11=1,043,490; 2024-03=704,068; 2024-11=1,503,018). NUMERATORS come from three routes, in era order: (1) the CA SoS per-county reporting-status page (vote.sos.ca.gov/returns/status), whose 'FENU' (final election-night unofficial) row carries the county's last election-night Ballots Cast and is bracketed by the next capture's switch to 'CCU' (county canvass update): 2008-11, 2010-11, 2014-11; (2) the pre-livevoterturnout GEMS-style feed sdvote.com/content/rov/electioninfo/election.xml, whose only election-night-adjacent Wayback capture is for the June 2016 primary: 2016-06; (3) the SOE/Scytl livevoterturnout.com system, which has served SD's live results since 2018 (scheme livevoterturnout.com/SanDiego/LiveResults/en/Index_N.html for 2018-2021, then livevoterturnout.com/ENR/sandiegocaenr/N/en/Index_N.html from the June 2022 primary on): 2018-06, 2018-11, 2022-06, 2022-11, 2024-03, 2024-11. The livevoterturnout pages are JS-rendered and expose an internal 'Website Updated' data timestamp plus 'Ballots Cast'/'Ballots Counted', so the plateau is identified by reading the highest cumulative total whose data timestamp holds steady from the post-midnight election-night update until the next-day ~5 PM canvass update; they were rendered via headless Chrome on the Wayback captures (web.archive.org's playback host throttles bursts, so renders used per-URL retry/backoff and the Bash sandbox had to be disabled for Chrome to reach the network). The status-page and election.xml routes are plain text and need no rendering. TEN SOURCED NUMERATORS (date / ballots / certified / pct, all confidence 'primary'): 2008-11-04 980,234 / 1,245,947 = 78.67%; 2010-11-02 663,326 / 926,363 = 71.61%; 2014-11-04 509,214 / 692,434 = 73.54%; 2016-06-07 468,340 / 775,930 = 60.36%; 2018-06-05 406,501 / 673,640 = 60.34%; 2018-11-06 536,734 / 1,173,924 = 45.72% (pre-vote-center, only 68% of precincts counted overnight); 2022-06-07 416,748 / 674,608 = 61.78%; 2022-11-08 565,982 / 1,043,490 = 54.24%; 2024-03-05 425,572 / 704,068 = 60.44%; 2024-11-05 975,373 / 1,503,018 = 64.89%. FOUR ROWS REMAIN NULL (confidence 'none'), all pre-2018: 2012-06-05, 2012-11-06, 2014-06-03, 2016-11-08. Each of those rows' own notes records the routes checked and exhausted; in summary, SD did not use livevoterturnout before 2018, its 2012-2016 results page was an unarchived JS viewer of election.xml (the feed has no Wayback capture on those election nights), and the PRESS-RELEASE ROUTE is EMPTY for them (the registrar's own news page, sdvote.com/content/rov/en/elections/news.html, is archived only from 2019; the County News Center, launched ~2014, ran only 'Election Results, When to Expect Them' previews plus an 'Election results available at SDVote.com' pointer, Wayback ts 20161114190143). Per the metric they are left null rather than substituting a different report-time or denominator. ADOPTION (per task): e-pollbooks 2022, so 2008 through 2018 = pre and 2022/2024 = post; automated signature verification (ASV) 2024, so every row except the two 2024 rows = pre. INTERPRETIVE CAVEAT: with the pre-2018 rows recovered, this panel does NOT show a tech-driven speed-up, and the confounds dominate. The highest election-night shares in the series are the OLDEST, pre-adoption generals (2008 78.67%, 2014 73.54%, 2010 71.61%), when nearly all ballots were cast at precincts and counted the same night; the share then collapses to 45.72% in 2018 as late-arriving vote-by-mail grew, and only partially recovers after adoption (2022 midterm 54.24%, 2024 presidential 64.89%). Like-to-like midterms run 71.61%/73.54% (pre) then 45.72% (2018, pre) then 54.24% (2022, post), i.e. the post-e-pollbook midterm sits well BELOW the pre-adoption midterms; like-to-like primaries are nearly flat across the boundary (2016-06 60.36% and 2018-06 60.34% pre vs 2022-06 61.78% and 2024-03 60.44% post). The dominant drivers are San Diego's shift to heavy, late-arriving VBM and the precinct-to-vote-center transition (2020), not e-pollbooks or ASV; any tech effect is at most the modest post-2018 recovery and cannot be separated from those changes with this panel.
